bylaws.title: Off Road Vehicle & All-terrain Vehicles
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows ATVs and off-road vehicles with 3+ wheels to drive on Township roads. You must follow provincial traffic rules and safety requirements.
ai_key_points: ATVs and off-road vehicles with 3+ wheels can drive on Township roads
Vehicles must have low-pressure tires (maximum 70 kpa/10 psi)
Must follow provincial Highway Traffic Act rules and regulations
Penalties follow provincial Highway Traffic Act if you break the rules

bylaws.title: Sale of Land Policy
bylaws.ai_summary: This bylaw sets rules for how Nipissing Township can sell municipal land. It requires appraisals, public notice, and council approval before any land sales.
ai_key_points: Council must declare land 'surplus' before it can be sold
Township must get professional appraisal before selling land
Public must be notified before any land sales happen
Some small parcels like narrow strips don't need appraisals
Township can sell by tender, real estate agent, or direct sale

bylaws.title: Trailer Use (Temporary during Construction)
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates rules for using trailers as temporary homes while building your permanent house in Nipissing Township.
ai_key_points: You can live in a trailer temporarily while constructing your permanent residence
Must follow Township policy requirements for trailer use during construction
Policy details are in attached schedules not included in this summary
By-law passed September 13, 2011

bylaws.title: Entrance 911 By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This bylaw requires all properties in Nipissing Township to display municipal address numbers on standardized signs for 911 emergency services. Property owners must maintain these signs or pay replacement costs.
ai_key_points: All properties with entrances or buildings must display assigned municipal address numbers
Signs must be specific green and white reflective aluminum signs visible from the road
Township installs initial signs but charges cost to property owner's permits
Property owners must maintain signs in good condition or pay replacement costs
Unpaid sign costs get added to your property tax bill after 30 days

bylaws.title: Authorize Speed Limit Changes
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets a 60 km/h speed limit on all Township roads that don't already have posted speed limit signs.
ai_key_points: All unposted Township roads now have a 60 km/h speed limit
Only applies to roads owned by the Township of Nipissing
Council held a public meeting before making this decision
Breaking this speed limit carries the same penalties as other Highway Traffic Act violations

bylaws.title: Amend BL 1088 – Off Road Vehicles on Municipal Roads
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows off-road vehicles like ATVs to drive on Township of Nipissing municipal roads with specific speed limits and equipment requirements.
ai_key_points: Off-road vehicles are allowed on municipal roads in Nipissing Township
Speed limit is 20 km/h on roads with 50 km/h limit, 50 km/h on faster roads
Vehicles must meet provincial equipment and safety requirements
Cannot drive on parks, beaches, cemeteries, or community centres
Breaking these rules is an offence with legal penalties

bylaws.title: Minimum Maintenance Standards – Roads
bylaws.ai_summary: Adopts minimum maintenance standards for municipal roads and highways as required by Ontario regulation. Defines plowing timelines, pothole repair schedules, and road surface conditions the township must meet.
ai_key_points: Sets snow clearing timelines after storms
Defines acceptable road surface conditions
Pothole repair timelines by road classification
Protects township from liability when standards are met
Based on Ontario Regulation 366/18

bylaws.title: Amending Maximum Weight on Bridges
bylaws.ai_summary: Amends the maximum weight limits permitted on township bridges based on engineering assessments. Restricts heavy vehicles from crossing bridges that cannot safely support their weight.
ai_key_points: Weight limits posted at each affected bridge
Based on structural engineering assessments
Heavy vehicles must use alternate routes
Fines for exceeding posted weight limits
Protects bridges from structural damage

bylaws.title: Boundary Road Agreement – Powassan
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township of Nipissing made an agreement with Powassan about managing a road that sits on the border between both municipalities.
ai_key_points: Creates an agreement between Nipissing Township and Powassan for a shared boundary road
Establishes which municipality is responsible for maintaining the road
Sets out cost-sharing arrangements for road upkeep and repairs
Defines jurisdiction and authority for the boundary road area

bylaws.title: Public Dock By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law regulates how residents can use three public boat docks in Nipissing Township, setting time limits and usage rules to keep docks accessible for everyone.
ai_key_points: Boats can only dock for 4 hours maximum during daytime (7am-11pm)
No overnight parking of boats at docks
Materials can only be stored for 1 hour while loading/unloading
Commercial boats need township permission before using docks
Fines range from $150-$400 for violations

bylaws.title: Landfill By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Governs the use of the township landfill site, including hours of operation, accepted materials, and tipping fees. Residents must show proof of residency to access the facility.
ai_key_points: Proof of residency required for landfill access
Hazardous waste and certain materials prohibited
Tipping fees apply to construction debris and large loads
Seasonal hours of operation posted at the site
Illegal dumping subject to fines and cleanup costs

bylaws.title: Amending Minimum Maintenance Standards Schedule E
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township updated its snow plowing rules to remove specific truck assignments from plow routes. This gives the Township more flexibility to respond to winter storms efficiently.
ai_key_points: Updates the Township's snow plowing schedule and routes
Removes requirement to assign specific trucks to specific routes
Allows Township to respond more flexibly to winter weather events
Amends previous by-law from 2019 about road maintenance standards

bylaws.title: Recreation Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: Township council creates a Recreation Committee to run recreational programs and manage the Community Centre at 2381 Highway 654.
ai_key_points: Committee has 2 council members and 4-8 residents appointed yearly
Committee manages Community Centre including fitness center and sets usage fees
Committee must submit annual budget to council by February 1st
Committee must hold at least 4 meetings per year
Committee can make rules for recreation programs and facility use

bylaws.title: Cemetery Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This bylaw creates a Cemetery Committee to manage the township's three cemeteries. The committee has five members and must follow provincial burial laws.
ai_key_points: Creates a 5-member committee (2 councillors, 3 residents) to run three township cemeteries
Committee must submit annual budget to council by February 1st each year
Committee must hold at least one meeting per year
Committee can only spend money approved in their budget or by council
Committee must follow provincial funeral and burial laws

bylaws.title: Community Safety Zone
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township created a Community Safety Zone with reduced speed limits and doubled fines for speeding violations around a school area.
ai_key_points: Speed limit is 40 km/h on Beatty Street, Front Street, Blake Street, and Powerplant Road
Area includes an elementary school, public park, and ice rink facility
Speeding fines are doubled in this zone compared to regular roads
OPP can enforce speed limits and issue tickets in this area

bylaws.title: Parking By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes parking regulations on township roads and public areas. Restricts overnight parking during winter months to allow for snow clearing operations and sets time limits in designated areas.
ai_key_points: No overnight parking on roads from November to April
Parking restrictions support winter snow removal
Time-limited parking in designated public areas
Vehicles in violation may be towed at owner expense
Fines set for each parking infraction

bylaws.title: Unopened Road Allowances By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law controls what you can and cannot do on unopened road allowances in Nipissing Township. You need permission before using these areas for any purpose.
ai_key_points: Prohibits unauthorized use of unopened road allowances without township permission
Requires permits for activities like building, parking, or storing items on these lands
Violations can result in fines and orders to remove unauthorized structures or materials
Township can enter property to inspect and enforce compliance with the rules

bylaws.title: Unauthorized Disposal of Waste
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law prohibits dumping garbage, waste, or debris on public or private property without permission. Violators can face fines and must clean up the mess.
ai_key_points: Cannot dump any waste, garbage, or debris on roads, parks, or other people's property
Includes household trash, construction materials, appliances, and hazardous substances
Property owners must clean up unauthorized waste dumped on their land
Fines range from hundreds to thousands of dollars depending on violation severity
Township can remove waste and charge cleanup costs to the violator

bylaws.title: Depositing of Snow on any Highway Or Bridge
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law prohibits residents from putting snow onto roads, highways, or bridges in Nipissing Township. Violators can be fined for blocking traffic or creating safety hazards.
ai_key_points: You cannot dump, push, or place snow on any road, highway, or bridge
This includes snow from driveways, parking lots, and private property
Breaking this rule can result in fines and penalties
The ban helps keep roads safe and clear for traffic

bylaws.title: Amend Zoning By-Law 2020-20 Section 3.44 Trailers
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law updates the rules about trailers in Nipissing Township. It changes the existing zoning rules from 2020 that control where and how trailers can be used.
ai_key_points: Updates existing trailer regulations in the township's zoning by-law
Modifies Section 3.44 which governs trailer use and placement
Changes rules from the original 2020 zoning by-law
Affects where trailers can be located on properties

bylaws.title: Culvert/Entrance
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for installing driveways that connect to municipal roads in Nipissing Township. Property owners must get approval and follow specific standards for culverts and drainage.
ai_key_points: You must get township approval before installing any driveway entrance to a municipal road
Culverts must be new, at least 400mm diameter, 9 metres long, and meet specific materials standards
Only one entrance per home is allowed, and you pay all installation costs
You must complete work within 6 months or permit expires; final inspection required
If you damage roads or don't comply, township will fix it and bill you

bylaws.title: Procedural By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes rules and procedures for council meetings, including how agendas are set, how motions are made, public participation rules, and rules of order for elected officials.
ai_key_points: Sets regular meeting schedule for council
Defines rules for public delegations and deputations
Outlines how motions and amendments are handled
Establishes quorum requirements
Governs closed session procedures

bylaws.title: Cemetery Regulations
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for Township of Nipissing cemeteries, covering burials, monuments, maintenance, and visitor conduct to ensure respectful cemetery operations.
ai_key_points: Cemetery hours, burial procedures, and grave opening/closing requirements are regulated
Monument size, materials, and installation must meet township specifications
Cemetery maintenance standards and groundskeeping rules are established
Visitor behavior rules including prohibited activities and respect requirements
Penalties and fines apply for violations of cemetery regulations

bylaws.title: Dog By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Regulates dog ownership in Nipissing Township, including licensing requirements, leash rules, and noise provisions. Establishes penalties for owners of dogs that cause disturbances or pose a threat to public safety.
ai_key_points: All dogs must be licensed annually with the township
Dogs must be leashed when off owner property
Owners liable for damage caused by their dog
Maximum number of dogs per household may apply
Fines for barking complaints and at-large violations

bylaws.title: Boundary Road Agreement – Callander
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates an agreement with the Municipality of Callander about maintaining and managing a shared boundary road between the two communities.
ai_key_points: Establishes formal agreement between Nipissing Township and Callander Municipality
Sets rules for maintaining shared boundary road infrastructure
Defines responsibilities for road repairs and upkeep costs
Creates framework for cooperation on boundary road management

bylaws.title: Accountability & Transparency
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for how Township of Nipissing officials must be open and accountable to residents about their decisions and use of public money.
ai_key_points: Township officials must publicly report on finances and decisions
Meeting records and documents must be available to residents
Public notice required before making important decisions
Complaint process established for transparency violations
Penalties may apply for officials who don't follow these rules

bylaws.title: Delegation of Authority
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows Township Council to give certain decision-making powers to staff members like the CAO, department heads, and other employees for daily operations.
ai_key_points: Council can delegate specific powers and duties to township staff members
Staff can make certain decisions without needing full council approval
Council retains oversight and can revoke delegated authority when needed
Helps streamline township operations and administrative processes

bylaws.title: Regulate Sale of Land
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for selling land in Nipissing Township. Property owners must follow specific procedures and requirements when selling their land.
ai_key_points: Property owners must get township approval before selling land
All land sales must follow specific application and documentation requirements
Violations can result in fines and legal action by the township
The by-law protects the community's interests in land transactions

bylaws.title: Nuisance Beaver By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Allows property owners to trap or remove nuisance beavers that are causing flooding or property damage, subject to notification to the township and compliance with provincial wildlife regulations.
ai_key_points: Property owners may remove beavers causing damage
Township must be notified before removal
Must comply with provincial trapping regulations
Beaver dam removal permitted to prevent flooding
Township may assist with removal in some cases

bylaws.title: Recreation Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes a Recreation Committee for Nipissing Township to oversee parks, recreation facilities, and community programs. The committee will make recommendations to council on recreational matters.
ai_key_points: Creates a Recreation Committee to advise council on parks and recreation matters
Committee members are appointed by council for set terms
Committee oversees recreational facilities, programs, and community events
Committee must follow specific meeting procedures and report to council

bylaws.title: Museum Board
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes a Museum Board for the Township of Nipissing to oversee local museum operations and cultural heritage activities.
ai_key_points: Creates an official Museum Board to manage township museum services
Sets rules for board member appointments and terms of service
Defines the board's powers and responsibilities for museum operations
Establishes meeting requirements and governance procedures for the board

bylaws.title: Cemetery Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes a Cemetery Committee for the Township of Nipissing to oversee local cemetery operations and management decisions.
ai_key_points: Creates a Cemetery Committee to manage township cemeteries
Sets out committee member appointments and terms
Defines committee responsibilities and meeting requirements
Establishes rules for cemetery oversight and decision-making

bylaws.title: Establishing and Regulating Fire Department
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes the Township of Nipissing Fire Department and sets rules for how it operates, including staffing, services, and cost recovery.
ai_key_points: Creates the Township of Nipissing Fire Department with a Fire Chief in charge
New firefighters must complete a one-year probation period and required training
Fire Chief can discipline, suspend, or recommend dismissal of firefighters for rule violations
Property owners may be charged for emergency response costs and special equipment
Department provides fire suppression, prevention, education, rescue and emergency services

bylaws.title: Social Media Policy
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for how Township employees and officials use social media when representing the municipality or discussing Township business.
ai_key_points: Township staff must follow guidelines when posting about work on social media
Personal social media use shouldn't conflict with Township duties or reputation
Confidential Township information cannot be shared on social media platforms
Violations may result in disciplinary action or termination for employees

bylaws.title: Code of Conduct
bylaws.ai_summary: Establishes a code of conduct for members of council and local boards, setting ethical standards for behaviour, conflicts of interest, and use of public resources.
ai_key_points: Council members must declare conflicts of interest
Prohibits use of office for personal benefit
Sets standards for respectful conduct
Complaints handled by the Integrity Commissioner
Penalties range from reprimand to suspension of pay

bylaws.title: Trailer Licensing By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: Requires licensing for trailers used as temporary or seasonal dwellings on private property. Sets conditions for trailer placement including lot size, servicing, and duration limits.
ai_key_points: Trailers on private lots require a township license
Annual license must be renewed
Minimum lot and setback requirements apply
Trailer must meet basic health and safety standards
License can be revoked for non-compliance

bylaws.title: provide for an Interim Tax Levy
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township of Nipissing is setting temporary property tax rates for 2024 until the final budget and tax rates are approved later in the year.
ai_key_points: Sets interim property tax rates for early 2024 before final budget approval
Allows township to collect property taxes while preparing the annual budget
Temporary rates will be adjusted when final 2024 tax rates are set

Committee will handle emergency planning and response coordination
